questions.questionText:
At 527°C the reaction :

NH3 (g) $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ $ \frac{1}{2} $ N2 (g) + $ \frac{3}{2} $ H2 (g) has $K_c = 4$

What is $K_p$ for the reaction

N2 (g) + 3H2 (g) $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ 2NH3 (g) :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Ammmonium hydrogen sulphide dissociates according to the equation

NH4HS(s) $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ NH3(g) + H2S(g)

The total pressure at equilibrium at 400 K is found to be 1 atm.

The equilibrium constant $K_p$ of the reaction is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement-I : The melting point of ice decreases with increase of pressure.

Statement-II : Ice contracts on melting.
options: Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true ; Statement-II is correct explanation for Statement-I.
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true; Statement-II is NOT a correct explanation for statement-I
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is false
Statement-I is false, Statement-II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A vessel at 1000 K contains CO2 with a pressure of 0.5 atm. Some of the CO2 is converted into CO on the addition of graphite. The value of equilibrium constant if the total pressure at equilibrium is 0.8 atm is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The exothermic formation of ClF3 is represented by the equation

Cl2(g) + 3F2(g) $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ 2ClF3(g); ΔHf = -329 kJ

Which of the following will increase the quantity of ClF3 in an equilibrium mixture of Cl2, F2 and ClF3?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement-I : The equilibrium of $\mathrm{A}(\mathrm{g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{B}(\mathrm{g})+\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g})$ is not affected by changing the volume.

Statement-II : $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ for the reaction does not depend on volume of the container.
options: Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true ; Statement-II is correct explanation for Statement-I.
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true; Statement-II is NOT a correct explanation for statement-I
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is false
Statement-I is false, Statement-II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In the reversible reaction $\mathrm{A} + \mathrm{B} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}$, if at a fixed temperature the concentrations of both reactants are made twice, then the equilibrium constant will
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement-I : For the reaction $\mathrm{A}(\mathrm{g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{B}(\mathrm{g})+\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g}), \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}=1 \mathrm{~atm}$. If we start with equal moles of all gases at 9 atm of initial pressure, then at equilibrium partial pressure of $A$ increases.

Statement-II : Reaction quotient $Q_p>K_p$ hence equilibrium shifts in backward direction.
options: Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true ; Statement-II is correct explanation for Statement-I.
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true; Statement-II is NOT a correct explanation for statement-I
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is false
Statement-I is false, Statement-II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The equilibrium constants for a few reactions are listed below. For which case will the reaction proceed to the greatest extent towards products (i.e., be closest to completion)?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement-I : For the reaction $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{g})$ if the volume of vessel is reduced to half of its original volume, equilibrium concentration of all gases will be doubled.

Statement-II : According to Le Chatelier's principle, reaction shifts in a direction that tends to minimized the effect of the stress.
options: Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true ; Statement-II is correct explanation for Statement-I.
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true; Statement-II is NOT a correct explanation for statement-I
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is false
Statement-I is false, Statement-II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement-I : Equilibrium constant for the reverse reaction is the inverse of the equilibrium constant for the reaction in the forward direction.

Statement-II : Equilibrium constant depends upon the way in which the reaction is written.
options: Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true ; Statement-II is correct explanation for Statement-I.
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is true; Statement-II is NOT a correct explanation for statement-I
Statement-I is true, Statement-II is false
Statement-I is false, Statement-II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the equilibrium reaction at a fixed temperature: $2CO(g) \rightleftharpoons CO_2(g) + C(\text{graphite})$, the equilibrium constant $K_p$ is $0.1 \, \mathrm{atm}^{-1}$. If the total pressure of the gaseous mixture at equilibrium is $1.1$ atm, find the equilibrium ratio of the partial pressures of CO and CO$_2$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At equilibrium, for the system water $\rightleftharpoons$ water vapour, what happens if the pressure is increased?
options: The boiling point of water will increase.
The boiling point of water will decrease.
The boiling point of water will remain unchanged.
Boiling point has no relation with pressure.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
X : 2SO2 + O2 $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ 2SO3

Y : PCl5 $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ PCl3 + Cl2

Z : 2HI $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ H2 + I2

Ratio of $K_p$ and $K_c$ (i.e., $\dfrac{K_p}{K_c}$) in the increasing order is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
For the equilibrium,

$\ce{N2O4(g) <=> 2NO2(g)}$

$x = \frac{D-d}{d}$ where d is experimental vapour density and D is theoretical vapour density. Thus, for the equilibrium

$\ce{NO2(g) <=> \frac{1}{2}N2O4(g)}$, x is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: CCl4(g) dissociates 50% into gaseous free radicals at 1000 K and at equilibrium experimental vapour density is found to be 38.5. Thus, CCl4(g) dissociates under the given conditions to
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
At 900 K and 1 atm, $SO_3(g)$ undergoes partial dissociation to form $SO_2(g)$ and $O_2(g)$ according to:

$SO_3(g) \rightleftharpoons SO_2(g) + \frac{1}{2} O_2(g)$

The density of the gaseous mixture at equilibrium is measured as 0.866 g dm$^{-3}$. The degree of dissociation of $SO_3(g)$ is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the reaction N2(g) + O2(g) $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ 2NO(g), the value of the equilibrium constant at 2000 K is $4.0 \times 10^{-4}$. When a catalyst is added, the system reaches equilibrium 10 times more quickly. Hence, the equilibrium constant at 2000 K in the presence of the catalyst will be
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
An amount of 4 moles of NH3 gas is introduced with a previously evacuated 1 L container in which it is partially dissociated at high temperature as

$2\text{NH}_3(g) \rightleftharpoons \text{N}_2(g) + 3\text{H}_2(g)$

At equilibrium, 2 mole of NH3(g) remained. The value of $K_C$ for the reaction is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
At temperature T, a compound AB2(g) dissociates according to the reaction

2AB2(g) $ \rightleftharpoons $ 2AB(g) + B2(g)

with a degree of dissociation ‘x’ which is small as compared to unity. The expression for Kp, in terms of ‘x’ and total pressure ‘P’ is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Calculate partial pressure of $B$ at equilibrium in the following equilibrium

$$ A(s) \rightleftharpoons B(g)+2 C(g) ; K_p=32 \mathrm{~atm}^3 . $$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For an equilibrium reaction $\mathrm{A}+2 \mathrm{~B} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{D} ; \mathrm{A}$ and B are mixed in a reaction vessel at 300 K . The initial concentration of B was 1.5 times the initial concentration of A . After the equilibrium, the equilibrium concentrations of A and D are same. Calculate $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{C}}$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A compound $\mathrm{A}_3 \mathrm{~B}_4(\mathrm{~g})$ dissociates according to the reaction:

$\mathrm{A}_3 \mathrm{~B}_4(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{AB}(\mathrm{g})+\mathrm{A}(\mathrm{g})+2 \mathrm{~B}(\mathrm{~g})$ with degree of dissociation $\alpha$, which is negligible in comparison to unity.

Total pressure $p$ is $x \sqrt{\frac{K_p}{16 P^4}}$. Find the value of $x$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
$\mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_3\right)_2$ is strongly heated and after equilibrium is attained, temperature changed to 298 K .

$$ \begin{gathered} \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_3\right)_2(s) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{CaCO}_3(s)+\mathrm{CO}_2(g)+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(g) \\ K_p=36(\text { pressure taken in atm }) \end{gathered} $$

Thus, pressure set up due to $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is $\_\_\_\_$ atm .
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The percent dissociation of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~g})$ if 0.1 mole of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ is kept in 0.4 L vessel at 1000 K for the reaction $2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{S}_2(\mathrm{~g}) ; K_{\mathrm{C}}=1.0 \times 10^{-6}$.
questions.subject: Chemistry
